1707	\begin{lemma}
1708	[Objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms of presheaf-of-module complexes]
1709	  \label{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation}
1710	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.quasiIso_of_evaluation,
1711	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_Fmap_homologyMap,
1712	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_of_evaluation}
1713	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1714	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1715	  % (proof, the sectionwise-reduction step) (read from
1716	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1220--1226).
1717	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended
1718	  % complex over any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex."
1719	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1720	  (sectionwise reduction).}
1721	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of rings on a category \(C\) and let
1722	  \(\varphi : K \to L\) be a morphism of chain complexes in the abelian category
1723	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. Suppose that for \emph{every}
1724	  object \(V\) the evaluated morphism — the image of \(\varphi\) under the functor
1725	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\bigr).
1726	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\), a morphism of complexes of
1727	  \(R(V)\)-modules — is a quasi-isomorphism. Then \(\varphi\) is a
1728	  quasi-isomorphism. This is the objectwise-reduction step that turns the
1729	  resolution claim of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} into a family of
1730	  sectionwise (combinatorial) statements: homology in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) is
1731	  computed open-by-open, because the evaluation functors
1732	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) are exact (hence preserve
1733	  homology) and jointly conservative: a morphism in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) is an isomorphism
1734	  iff each of its evaluations is an isomorphism, and each evaluation functor preserves
1735	  homology.
1736	\end{lemma}
1737	
1738	\begin{proof}
1739	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1740	  A morphism of complexes is a quasi-isomorphism iff it induces an isomorphism on
1741	  homology in every degree (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\),
1742	  \(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_iff\_isIso\_homologyMap}\)). Fix a degree \(i\). The
1743	  homology map \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) iff each of
1744	  its evaluations is an isomorphism, since the underlying
1745	  presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of
1746	  presheaves is an isomorphism iff it is so objectwise (joint conservativity). For
1747	  each \(V\), evaluation preserves homology, so the evaluation of \(H_i(\varphi)\)
1748	  is the homology map of the evaluated complex morphism; the latter is an
1749	  isomorphism because the evaluated morphism is a quasi-isomorphism by hypothesis.
1750	  Hence \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism for all \(i\), i.e.\ \(\varphi\) is a
1751	  quasi-isomorphism.
1752	\end{proof}

9508	  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes, \(V \subseteq X\) an open, and
9509	  \(\Phi = \operatorname{pushforwardEquivOfIso}\varphi : X.\mathrm{Modules} \simeq Y.\mathrm{Modules}\)
9510	  the induced equivalence. Then the pushforward carries the corepresenting object of
9511	  Definition~\ref{def:jshriek_ou} along the isomorphism:
9512	  \[
9513	    \Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_V\bigr)
9514	      \;\cong\;
9515	    j_!\mathcal{O}_{\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,V},
9516	  \]
9517	  where \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,V\) is the preimage of \(V\) under \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}\), i.e.\
9518	  the image open of \(V\) in \(Y\).
9519	\end{lemma}
9520	\begin{proof}
9521	  \uses{def:jshriek_ou, lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso, lem:compCoyonedaIso_mathlib,
9522	    lem:coyoneda_fullyFaithful_mathlib}
9523	  The argument is a corepresentability transport; \(j_!\mathcal{O}_V\) is never unfolded. Write
9524	  \(A = j_!\mathcal{O}_V\). It suffices, by full faithfulness of \(\operatorname{coyoneda}\)
9525	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:coyoneda_fullyFaithful_mathlib}, which reflects isomorphisms), to produce a natural
9526	  isomorphism of the corepresented functors
9527	  \(\operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}(A))) \cong
9528	  \operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}V}))\); the desired
9529	  isomorphism of objects is then its preimage.
9530	
9531	  We build that natural isomorphism by chaining three identifications.
9532	  \begin{enumerate}
9533	    \item The equivalence–coyoneda isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:compCoyonedaIso_mathlib}, applied to the
9534	      adjunction underlying \(\Phi\), gives
9535	      \(\operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}(A))) \cong
9536	      \Phi.\mathrm{inverse} \circ \operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}\,A)\).
9537	    \item The corepresentability isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso}, whiskered by the
9538	      forgetful functor to types, identifies the corepresented functor with the sections functor:
9539	      \(\operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}\,A) \cong \operatorname{sectionsFunctor} V\) (as
9540	      \(\mathrm{Type}\)-valued functors). This rests on the corepresentability of \(\Gamma(V,-)\) by
9541	      \(j_!\mathcal{O}_V\) of Definition~\ref{def:jshriek_ou}.
9542	    \item Sections of a pushforward are, by definition, sections over the preimage open:
9543	      \(\Phi.\mathrm{inverse} \circ \operatorname{sectionsFunctor} V =
9544	      \operatorname{sectionsFunctor}(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}V)\), an equality of functors. Reading
9545	      Lemma~\ref{lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso} backwards at the open \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}V\) then
9546	      identifies this with \(\operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}V}))\).
9547	  \end{enumerate}
9548	  Composing (1)–(3) yields the required natural isomorphism of corepresented functors, and
9549	  Lemma~\ref{lem:coyoneda_fullyFaithful_mathlib} reflects it to the stated isomorphism of objects. No
9550	  adjunction-mate transposition or naturality square is computed by hand: the only geometric inputs are
9551	  the two definitional equalities ``corepresented functor \(=\) sections functor'' and ``sections of a
9552	  pushforward \(=\) sections over the preimage''.
9553	\end{proof}

9575	\begin{lemma}[Pushforward along an iso commutes with restriction to an open]
9576	  \label{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction}
9577	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_commutes_restriction}
9578	  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
9579	  \uses{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib, lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}
9580	  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes,
9581	  \(\Phi = \operatorname{pushforwardEquivOfIso}\varphi\) the induced equivalence of module categories,
9582	  and \(V \subseteq X\) an open with image \(V' = \varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,V \subseteq Y\). The
9583	  homeomorphism underlying \(\varphi\) restricts to a homeomorphism \(V \cong V'\), hence to an
9584	  equivalence of the opens-sites \(\operatorname{Opens} V \simeq \operatorname{Opens} V'\); let
9585	  \[
9586	    e : (X.\mathrm{Modules} \!\restriction\! V) \;\simeq\; (Y.\mathrm{Modules} \!\restriction\! V')
9587	  \]
9588	  be the induced equivalence of restricted-module categories obtained from the site-equivalence
9589	  transport of Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}. Then for every
9590	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(H\) there is a comparison isomorphism
9591	  \[
9592	    e.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(H.\operatorname{over} V\bigr)
9593	      \;\cong\;
9594	    \bigl(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\bigr).\operatorname{over} V',
9595	  \]
9596	  identifying ``restrict to \(V\) then push forward over \(V'\)'' with ``push forward then restrict to
9597	  \(V'\)''.
9598	\end{lemma}
9599	\begin{proof}
9600	  \uses{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib, lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}
9601	  Both sides are computed open-by-open on the subspace site of \(V'\). For an open
9602	  \(W \subseteq V'\) with preimage \(W_0 = \varphi.\mathrm{hom}^{-1}\,W \subseteq V\), the sections of the
9603	  right-hand restriction are the sections of \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\) over the image
9604	  open \(W\), which — pushforward acting on opens by the inverse-image homeomorphism — are the sections
9605	  of \(H\) over \(W_0\) (the restriction-of-objects identity of Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib},
9606	  applied to the open immersions \(V \hookrightarrow X\) and \(V' \hookrightarrow Y\)). The same
9607	  sections compute the left-hand side: the site-equivalence transport
9608	  \(e\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib} relabels the open \(W\) of \(V'\)
9609	  to the corresponding open \(W_0\) of \(V\) and reads off \(H.\operatorname{over} V\) there. The two
9610	  presheaves of sections therefore agree, compatibly with restriction maps, and the comparison is a
9611	  morphism of sheaves of modules over \(V'\); being an isomorphism on every section group it is an
9612	  isomorphism. This is the open-restriction analogue of the \(\operatorname{bind}\) construction's
9613	  per-piece transport, with the homeomorphism-induced opens-site equivalence in place of the
9614	  iterated-slice equivalence.
9615	\end{proof}

9617	\begin{lemma}[Pushforward along an iso preserves quasi-coherence]
9618	  \label{lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}
9619	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}
9620	  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
9621	  \uses{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib,
9622	    lem:presentation_map_mathlib, lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib,
9623	    lem:nonempty_quasicoherentData_mathlib, lem:presentation_ofIsIso_mathlib,
9624	    lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}
9625	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Section ``Functoriality for quasi-coherent
9626	  % modules'', items (1),(7) and the closing remark on pushforward (read from
9627	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L4729--4767).
9628	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules $\mathcal{F}$ is
9629	  % quasi-coherent if and only if the restriction of $\mathcal{F}$
9630	  % to each affine open $U = \Spec(R)$ is of the form
9631	  % $\widetilde M$ for some $R$-module $M$."
9632	  % "Given a morphism of schemes $f : Y \to X$ the pullback
9633	  % of a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module is a quasi-coherent
9634	  % $\mathcal{O}_Y$-module. See
9635	  % Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-pullback-quasi-coherent}."
9636	  % "On the other hand, it is in general not the case that the
9637	  % pushforward of a quasi-coherent module is quasi-coherent.
9638	  % Here is a case where this does hold."
9639	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, ``Functoriality for quasi-coherent modules''.}
9640	  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes and \(H : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) a
9641	  quasi-coherent module. Then the pushforward \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\) along
9642	  \(\varphi\) is again quasi-coherent.
9643	\end{lemma}
9644	\begin{proof}
9645	  \uses{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib,
9646	    lem:presentation_map_mathlib, lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib,
9647	    lem:nonempty_quasicoherentData_mathlib, lem:presentation_ofIsIso_mathlib,
9648	    lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}
9649	  Quasi-coherence is a \emph{local} datum: it transports across the equivalence \(\Phi\) by moving
9650	  \(H\)'s local presentations to the image cover, member by member. We never seek a global presentation
9651	  of \(H\) (it has none — \(H\) is quasi-coherent only on the abstract source); we move the local
9652	  presentations one open at a time.
9653	
9654	  \emph{The cover.} Extract \(H\)'s quasi-coherence datum
9655	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:nonempty_quasicoherentData_mathlib}): a family \((U_i)_{i \in I}\) of opens covering
9656	  \(X\) together with, for each \(i\), a presentation \(p_i\) of \(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\). Form the
9657	  image cover \(V_i = \varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,U_i\) of \(Y\); it covers \(Y\) because the underlying
9658	  map of \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}\) is a homeomorphism, so the preimages of a cover form a cover (the image
9659	  of an affine open under the scheme isomorphism is again affine,
9660	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}, when one wants the cover to remain affine). By
9661	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib} it suffices to show that
9662	  \((\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H).\operatorname{over} V_i\) is quasi-coherent for every \(i\).
9663	
9664	  \emph{Per-member transport.} Fix \(i\) and let
9665	  \(e_i : (X.\mathrm{Modules}\!\restriction\! U_i) \simeq (Y.\mathrm{Modules}\!\restriction\! V_i)\) be
9666	  the opens-site equivalence induced by \(\varphi\) restricted to \(U_i\), built from
9667	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}. This \(e_i\) preserves colimits and carries
9668	  the structure-sheaf unit to the structure-sheaf unit (it is a site equivalence whose underlying base
9669	  is a homeomorphism), so by Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib} the presentation \(p_i\) of
9670	  \(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\) transports to a presentation of
9671	  \(e_i.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}(H.\operatorname{over} U_i)\). The comparison isomorphism of
9672	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction},
9673	  \[
9674	    e_i.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\bigr)
9675	      \;\cong\;
9676	    \bigl(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\bigr).\operatorname{over} V_i,
9677	  \]
9678	  then carries that presentation, via Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_ofIsIso_mathlib}, to a presentation of
9679	  \((\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H).\operatorname{over} V_i\). A sheaf of modules with a (global)
9680	  presentation is quasi-coherent, so each member of the image cover is quasi-coherent, and the claim
9681	  follows by Lemma~\ref{lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib}.
9682	
9683	  This is the isomorphism case of the Stacks functoriality remark (``it is in general not the case that
9684	  the pushforward of a quasi-coherent module is quasi-coherent''; here it holds because \(\varphi\) is an
9685	  isomorphism, equivalently \(\varphi_* = (\varphi^{-1})^*\) is a pullback).
9686	\end{proof}

7574	  is the \(n\)-fold product \(\prod_{k} Z_k\) of the same family taken in the slice category
7575	  \(\operatorname{Over} S\) --- i.e.\ the \(n\)-fold fibre power over \(S\) is the \(n\)-fold product
7576	  in \(\operatorname{Over} S\). In particular the fibre power has the clean recursion
7577	  \(\prod_{k : \mathrm{Fin}(p+2)} Z_k \cong Z_0 \times_S \prod_{k : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1)} Z_{k+1}\)
7578	  along \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+2) \cong \mathrm{Fin}(1) \oplus \mathrm{Fin}(p+1)\).
7579	\end{lemma}
7580	\begin{proof}
7581	  \uses{lem:widePullbackCone_isLimitOfFan_mathlib, lem:over_mkIdTerminal_mathlib}
7582	  In the slice \(\operatorname{Over} S\) the object \(\operatorname{Over.mk}(\mathrm{id}_S)\) is
7583	  terminal by Lemma~\ref{lem:over_mkIdTerminal_mathlib}, and the legs of the wide-pullback diagram
7584	  all land in this terminal base. By Lemma~\ref{lem:widePullbackCone_isLimitOfFan_mathlib} a
7585	  limiting fan of the legs is then a limit of the wide-pullback diagram, so the wide pullback over
7586	  \(S\) coincides with the product of the legs computed in \(\operatorname{Over} S\). The recursion
7587	  is the standard splitting of a finite product along \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+2) \cong \mathrm{Fin}(1)
7588	  \oplus \mathrm{Fin}(p+1)\) (a binary product with the head factor).
7589	\end{proof}

7653	  \label{lem:overProd_coproduct_distrib}

7659	  \uses{lem:prod_coproduct_distrib, lem:overProdLeftIsoPullback_mathlib}
7660	  Let \(\mathcal{C}\) be a finitary pre-extensive category with pullbacks and \(S \in \mathcal{C}\).
7661	  For a finite index type \(\iota : \operatorname{Type} 0\), a family \((A_i)_{i \in \iota}\) of
7662	  objects of \(\operatorname{Over} S\), and a single \(B \in \operatorname{Over} S\), the
7663	  \emph{binary product in the slice} distributes over the coproduct:
7664	  \[
7665	    \bigl(\textstyle\coprod_{i} A_i\bigr) \times B
7666	      \;\cong\;
7667	    \coprod_{i} \bigl(A_i \times B\bigr)

7669	  \]
7670	  where \(\times\) is the categorical binary product of \(\operatorname{Over} S\). This is the form in
7671	  which the inductive step of Lemma~\ref{lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower} consumes distributivity:
7672	  the recursion lives entirely in \(\operatorname{Over} S\), so it needs the slice binary product, not

7677	  The forgetful functor \(\operatorname{Over} S \to \mathcal{C}\) reflects isomorphisms: a morphism of
7678	  the slice is an isomorphism iff its underlying \(\mathcal{C}\)-morphism (its \(.\mathrm{left}\)) is.
7679	  It therefore suffices to identify the underlying objects. The coproduct in \(\operatorname{Over} S\)
7680	  is computed on underlying objects, \((\coprod_i A_i).\mathrm{left} = \coprod_i A_i.\mathrm{left}\),
7681	  and the underlying object of a binary product in \(\operatorname{Over} S\) is the fibre product of
7682	  the two structure maps, \((Y \times Z).\mathrm{left} \cong \operatorname{pullback} Y.\mathrm{hom}\,
7683	  Z.\mathrm{hom}\), via \(\operatorname{Over.prodLeftIsoPullback}\)
7684	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:overProdLeftIsoPullback_mathlib}). Under these identifications both sides become
7685	  the \(\mathcal{C}\)-level objects of the one-sided distributivity
7686	  Lemma~\ref{lem:prod_coproduct_distrib}: \((\coprod_i A_i.\mathrm{left}) \times_S B.\mathrm{left}\)
7687	  on the left and \(\coprod_i (A_i.\mathrm{left} \times_S B.\mathrm{left})\) on the right. Matching
7688	  \(.\mathrm{left}\) with that \(\mathcal{C}\)-level isomorphism, and noting all comparison maps commute
7689	  with the projection to \(S\), gives the slice isomorphism.
7690	\end{proof}

7692	\begin{lemma}
7693	[One-sided distributivity in \(\operatorname{Over} S\), product-on-the-right form]
7694	  \label{lem:overProd_coproduct_distrib_right}

7699	  and a family \((Y_i)_{i \in \iota}\) of objects of \(\operatorname{Over} S\), the slice binary
7700	  product distributes over the coproduct in the second argument:
7701	  \[
7702	    A \times \bigl(\textstyle\coprod_{i} Y_i\bigr)
7703	      \;\cong\;
7704	    \coprod_{i} \bigl(A \times Y_i\bigr)
7705	      \qquad\text{in } \operatorname{Over} S.
7706	  \]
7707	  This is the braiding-twin of Lemma~\ref{lem:overProd_coproduct_distrib}; the inductive step of
7708	  Lemma~\ref{lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower} uses it to distribute the head factor over the

7760	  (\mathrm{Fin}(p+2)\to\iota)\), \((i,\tau)\mapsto \operatorname{Fin.cons} i\,\tau\), reindexes that

7782	      \;\cong\;

7797	  and then instantiated at \(\mathcal{C} = \operatorname{Scheme}\), which is finitary extensive by
7798	  Lemma~\ref{lem:finitaryExtensive_scheme_mathlib}.

7809	    lem:overProd_coproduct_distrib_right, lem:finitaryExtensive_scheme_mathlib}
7810	  Induction on \(p\). Throughout, Lemma~\ref{lem:widePullback_overX_eq_prod} lets us treat the
7811	  \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power over \(S\) as the \((p+1)\)-fold product in \(\operatorname{Over} S\),
7812	  so the induction has the clean recursion \(P^{\times_S (p+2)} \cong P \times_S P^{\times_S (p+1)}\)
7813	  with \(P = \coprod_i X_i\).

7815	  \emph{Base step} \(p = 0\) is Lemma~\ref{lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower_zero}: the \(1\)-fold
7816	  fibre power is \(\coprod_i X_i\) itself, indexed by \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(1) \to \iota\).
7817

7835	  (via \(\operatorname{sigmaSigmaIso}\)) and reindexes the pairs \((i,\tau)\) as

7837	  \times_S Y_\tau\) with \(X_{\sigma(0)} \times_S \cdots \times_S X_{\sigma(p+1)}\). The structure

7901	  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}(\sigma \mapsto j_\sigma)\), where
7902	  \(j_\sigma : U_\sigma \hookrightarrow X\) is the open immersion of the intersection open
7903	  \(U_\sigma\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}. In words: the fibre power
7904	  of the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\) is the intersection open, and the disjoint
7905	  union over \(\sigma\) assembles the {\v C}ech backbone.
7906	\end{lemma}
7907	\begin{proof}
7908	  \uses{lem:cechBackbone_obj_widePullback, lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower,
7909	    lem:widePullback_overX_eq_prod, lem:widePullback_openImm_inter}
7910	  Assemble the chain in \(\operatorname{Over} X\). By
7911	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cechBackbone_obj_widePullback} the degree-\(p\) object \(Y_p\) is the wide
7912	  pullback \(\bigl(\coprod_i U_i\bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}\), the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power of
7913	  \(q\) over \(X\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower} (instantiated at
7914	  \(\mathcal{C} = \operatorname{Scheme}\), with \(S = X\) and the family
7915	  \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)) this fibre power is the coproduct
7916	  \(\coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I} \bigl(U_{\sigma(0)} \times_X \cdots \times_X
7917	  U_{\sigma(p)}\bigr)\) over the multi-indices. This decomposition delivers each \(\sigma\)-component in
7918	  its slice-product normal form, namely the iterated product
7919	  \(\prod^{\mathrm{c}}_{k}\operatorname{Over.mk}(U_{\sigma(k)} \hookrightarrow X)\) in
7920	  \(\operatorname{Over} X\); before it can be identified as an intersection open it must be converted
7921	  back to the wide-pullback form, which is exactly Lemma~\ref{lem:widePullback_overX_eq_prod} (the
7922	  iterated product over \(X\) in \(\operatorname{Over} X\) equals the wide fibre power over \(X\)). With
7923	  the component thus in wide-pullback form, Lemma~\ref{lem:widePullback_openImm_inter} applies: each
7924	  \(\sigma\)-component, being the wide fibre power over \(X\) of the open immersions
7925	  \(U_{\sigma(k)} \hookrightarrow X\), is the intersection open
7926	  \(U_\sigma = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma = \bigcap_k U_{\sigma(k)}\), with
7927	  structure map the open immersion \(j_\sigma\). Composing these
7928	  isomorphisms gives \(Y_p \cong \coprod_\sigma U_\sigma\); the structure-map agreement is
7929	  automatic, since each step's isomorphism commutes with the projection to \(X\), so the
7930	  universal map out of the coproduct is \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}(\sigma \mapsto j_\sigma)\).
7931	
7932	  \emph{Universe reduction.} The distributivity Lemma~\ref{lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower} and the
7933	  binary-distributivity leaf it rests on (Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_sigmaDesc_fst_mathlib}) require the
7934	  index type to lie in \(\operatorname{Type} 0\); the cover index \(I\) of \(\mathcal{U}\) is a
7935	  finite type that need not live in \(\operatorname{Type} 0\). The reduction is therefore performed
7936	  here: from finiteness of \(I\) choose an equivalence \(e : I \cong \mathrm{Fin}(n)\) (Mathlib's
7937	  \(\operatorname{Finite.equivFin}\), or \(\operatorname{Fintype.equivFin}\) once a \(\operatorname{Fintype}\)
7938	  instance is fixed), and reindex the cover family \((U_i \hookrightarrow X)\) and every coproduct- and
7939	  wide-pullback-index along it to land at \(\mathrm{Fin}(n) : \operatorname{Type} 0\). Two index
7940	  transports are involved. On the left, the source coproduct is reindexed along \(e\),
7941	  \[
7942	    \coprod_{i \in I} \mathcal{U}.X
7943	      \;\cong\;
7944	    \coprod_{j \in \mathrm{Fin}(n)} \bigl(\mathcal{U}.X \circ e^{-1}\bigr),
7945	  \]
7946	  the coproduct comparison induced by the index equivalence (\(\operatorname{Sigma.whiskerEquiv}\)).
7947	  On the right, the multi-index type is reindexed on its codomain,
7948	  \[
7949	    \bigl(\mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \mathrm{Fin}(n)\bigr)
7950	      \;\cong\;
7951	    \bigl(\mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I\bigr),
7952	  \]
7953	  the arrow-congruence \(\operatorname{Equiv.arrowCongr}\) applied to \(e^{-1}\) in the codomain, under
7954	  which the coproduct over \(\sigma\)-components of the \(\mathrm{Fin}(n)\)-decomposition matches the
7955	  coproduct over the original multi-indices. Apply the \(\operatorname{Type} 0\) decomposition of
7956	  Lemma~\ref{lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower} at \(\mathrm{Fin}(n)\), then transport the resulting
7957	  isomorphism back along these two equivalences. Reindexing a coproduct (resp.\ a wide fibre power)
7958	  along a bijection of index types is an isomorphism compatible with the structure maps, so the
7959	  transported isomorphism is again \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}(\sigma \mapsto j_\sigma)\) over \(X\).
7960	\end{proof}
7961	
7962	\begin{lemma}
7963	
7964	[The cover arrow is the coproduct of its member arrows in the slice]
7965	  \label{lem:coverArrow_over_sigma}
7966	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverSigmaIso,
7967	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverCofan,
7968	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverIsColimit,
7969	    CategoryTheory.overSigmaDescCofan,
7970	    CategoryTheory.overSigmaDescIsColimit,
7971	    CategoryTheory.overSigmaDescIso}
7972	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}
7973	  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an open cover with cover arrow
7974	  \(q = \operatorname{Sigma.desc}\mathcal{U}.f : \coprod_{i \in I} U_i \to X\). Then in the slice
7975	  category \(\operatorname{Over} X\) the object \(\operatorname{Over.mk}(q)\) carrying the cover arrow is
7976	  the coproduct of the member arrows \(\operatorname{Over.mk}(\mathcal{U}.f\,i)\):
7977	  \[
7978	    \operatorname{Over.mk}\bigl(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\mathcal{U}.f\bigr)
7979	      \;\cong\;
7980	    \coprod_{i \in I} \operatorname{Over.mk}(\mathcal{U}.f\,i)
7981	      \qquad\text{in } \operatorname{Over} X.
7982	  \]
7983	  This is the degree-\(0\) instance of the {\v C}ech backbone identification
7984	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma}): the source of the cover arrow is the disjoint union of the
7985	  cover members, structurally over \(X\).
7986	\end{lemma}
7987	\begin{proof}
7988	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}
7989	  Exhibit a colimit cocone explicitly. The cofan
7990	  \(\operatorname{coverArrowOverCofan}\) over the family \((\operatorname{Over.mk}(\mathcal{U}.f\,i))_i\)
7991	  has apex \(\operatorname{Over.mk}(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\mathcal{U}.f)\), with the \(i\)-th leg the
7992	  morphism in \(\operatorname{Over} X\) lifting the coproduct inclusion
7993	  \(\iota_i : U_i \to \coprod_j U_j\) (well-defined as an arrow over \(X\) because
7994	  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\mathcal{U}.f \circ \iota_i = \mathcal{U}.f\,i\), the triangle
7995	  \(\operatorname{Over.w}\)). It is a colimit (\(\operatorname{coverArrowOverIsColimit}\)) by
7996	  \(\operatorname{mkCofanColimit}\): the universal map out of the cofan is the
7997	  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\) of the components (\(\operatorname{Sigma.ι\_desc}\)), and uniqueness is
7998	  \(\operatorname{Sigma.hom\_ext}\), both lifted to the slice via \(\operatorname{Over.homMk}\) since the
7999	  forgetful functor \(\operatorname{Over} X \to \operatorname{Scheme}\) reflects the relevant equalities.
8000	  Comparing with \(\operatorname{coproductIsCoproduct}\) gives the asserted isomorphism
8001	  \(\operatorname{coverArrowOverSigmaIso}\).
8002	\end{proof}
8003	
8004	\begin{lemma}
8005	
8006	\leanok
8007	[Push--pull over the {\v C}ech backbone is the product over intersection opens]
8008	  \label{lem:pushPull_sigma_iso}
8009	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_sigma_iso}

# Strategy

## Goal

Prove `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`),
the protected, frozen-signature target: for `f : X ⟶ S` separated and quasi-compact, `F`
quasi-coherent, `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`, an isomorphism in the weak existence
form `Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F)` (under
`[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`), with `higherDirectImage f i F =
((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`. End-state: zero inline `sorry` in the cone, zero
project axioms, kernel-only axioms.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P5a-resolution `cechAugmented_exact` (augmented Čech complex resolves F) | ACTIVE (stubs) | ~2–5 | ~250–450 | Sub-brick A in `CechSectionIdentification.lean`: Stub 3 DONE; Stubs 5/6 re-signed to the AUGMENTED `D'_aug`; Stub-1 foundation `coproduct_distrib_fibrePower` DECOMPOSED into 4 `\uses`-linked leaves — all 4 leaves + Fin-succ split + Over-S helpers BUILT axiom-clean. Residual = inductive assembly `widePullback_coproduct_iso` (`overProd_coproduct_distrib` Over-S bridge + induction) + consumer `cechBackbone_left_sigma`, then Stubs 2/4/5/6. | **UNIVERSE TRAP:** `FinitaryPreExtensive.isIso_sigmaDesc_fst` is `{α : Type}` (Type 0) but `𝒰.I₀ : Type v` — the induction MUST run at `Type 0` and reindex `𝒰.I₀ ≃ Fin n` (via `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]`) at the consumer; naively widening the leaves to `Type*` BREAKS `isIso_sigmaDesc_fst`. Shared with dead `CechAcyclic.affine`. |
| P5a-consumer `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp` (affine open immersions are f_*-acyclic) | ACTIVE | ~2–4 | ~150–280 | **Need#2 CLOSED** (`affine_serre_vanishing_general_open`, axiom-clean — general-affine-open Serre vanishing, no restriction functor). Sole residual = **Need#1** jShriekOU scheme-iso transport: core `modulesIsoSpecExtTransport` DONE; the 5 decomposed sub-lemmas (`jshriek_transport_along_iso`, `pushforward_commutes_free`/`_sheafify`, `yoneda_transport_along_homeo`, `pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`) leandag frontier-ready. | Need#1 transport (commute pushforward with free/sheafification + yoneda) is the dominant wall; necessity RESOLVED (Open questions). Naive open-subscheme transport REJECTED (restriction-injectives WALL). |
| P5b comparison assembly | BLOCKED | ~2–3 | ~150–300 | P4 engine + P5a resolution + termwise `f_*`-acyclicity + EnoughInjectives connector. | Final Route-A assembly of the protected goal `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (line-780 sorry). Gated on P5a. |

## Completed

| Phase | Iters (done@ · used) | LOC | Files | Key results | Reusable techniques | Pitfalls |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 push–pull functor laws | 002 · 2 | ~120 | `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` | `pushPullMap_comp` closing push–pull functoriality | object-form align `simp [Functor.comp_obj]` before `reassoc_of%`; `rawPushPullMap`+`subst`+pentagon | `conjugateEquiv_comp` mate route INFEASIBLE (kernel `whnf` blow-up) |
| P2 `CechNerve`/`CechComplex` | 002 · 1 | ~60 | `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` | `pushPullFunctor`, `coverCechNerve(Aug)`, `CechNerve`, `CechComplex` axiom-clean | `Over.lift`+`.rightOp`+`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj`; terminal augmentation | none |
| P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (Leray, 015E) | 009 · 6 | ~965 | `AcyclicResolution.lean` | `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`, `rightDerivedOneIsoCokerOfAcyclic`, horseshoe `ofShortExact`, dimension shift — axiom-clean | decompose-then-build; two-step `cokernel.mapIso`; `Nat.rec` staircase off `stairGen` | `ShortComplex.mapCyclesIso` WRONG for left-exact functor; `← G.map_comp` silently fails by a mapped-complex term |
| P3 standard-cover Čech vanishing (section form, tilde) | 022 · ~14 | ~1200 | `CechAcyclic.lean` | `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` + `sectionCech_homology_exact` (IsZero homology p≥1) for `F=~M`; L1 tilde-bridge + L3 combinatorial core | accessors DEFEQ (`rfl`); abstract section maps (`set;clear_value`) before `IsLocalizedModule.ext`; target ABSOLUTE section complex | residual qcoh `F≅~ΓF` (01I8) deferred to 02KG consumer; old relative `affine` sorry superseded |
| P3b Čech↔derived bridge bricks | 025 · ~9 | ~1500 | `PresheafCech`,`FreePresheafComplex`,`CechBridge` | `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (free resolution of `O_𝒰`), `ses_cech_h1`, `injective_cech_acyclic` (pos-degree Čech vanishing for injectives) — axiom-clean | op-transport via `opFunctor`+`preadditiveYoneda`; `erw`+`.trans` for defeq-carrier mismatch; Route-B arrow-iso transfer | `maxHeartbeats 2000000` needed; LSP-staleness (trust only `lake env lean`) |
| Absolute-cohomology Form B + 01EO general basis criterion | 028 · 3 | ~600 | `AbsoluteCohomology`,`CechToCohomology` | `jShriekOU`=sheafify(free(yoneda U)); H⁰≅Γ (+naturality); 01EO chain L1–L4+top (`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`); `BasisCovSystem`/`HasVanishingHigherCech` cover-system encoding | Form B kills restriction-preserves-injectives (injective in 2nd Ext arg); cover-local presheaf form; `attribute [local instance] hasExtModules`; AB4* `Epi(Pi.map)` elementwise | `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` absent in Mathlib → carried as explicit hyp (P5a convention); 3 first-attempt iters, no churn |
| 01I8 `F≅~(ΓF)` via section-localization (Route B) | 048 · ~14 (040→048) | ~900 | `QcohTildeSections`,`QcohRestrictBasicOpen` | KEYSTONE `qcoh_section_isLocalizedModule` (ρ_f localizes Γ); Route-B assembly `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent` ⟹ **unconditional `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`**; tile chain B0–B4 + `qcoh_section_equalizer` + `isLocalizedModule_of_exact` | sheaf-axiom equalizer (NOT span-cover, which was circular); bundled `restrictScalars`-carrier for two-action tile descent; `change`/defeq for the `↑R`-Semiring diamond; basis-check via `IsCoverDense.iso_of_restrict_iso`+`specBasicOpen` | span-cover descent on global sections is CIRCULAR; tile lemma is NOT `restrict_obj`-rfl; `IsLocalizing`/`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizing` absent; over-budget (~14 iters vs est ~2) — the single hardest sub-route |
| 02KG affine Serre vanishing (cover-system + tops) | 052 · ~4 (049→052) | ~1100 | `AffineSerreVanishing`,`CechAcyclic` | unconditional `affine_serre_vanishing` + `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh`; residual `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` (route-B change-of-ring); `affineCoverSystem : BasisCovSystem` | the `private SectionCech*` core is `{R}[CommRing R]`-polymorphic → re-instantiate `dDiff_exact` over `R_f` at module level (no sheaf infra); discharge tops via `_of_tildeVanishing` reshaper | the residual covers a PROPER `D(f)` (cover spans `√f`, not `R`); plan-validate stop-marker/noop traps cost iters 050/051 |
| Need#2 general-affine-open Serre vanishing (`02KG` for arbitrary affine `V`) | 058 · 3 (056→058) | ~400 | `AffineSerreVanishing`,`CechAcyclic` | unconditional `affine_serre_vanishing_general_open` (`Ext^p(jShriekOU V,F)=0`, any affine open `V`); enlarged `affineCoverSystemGeneral` (basis = ALL affine opens) + change-of-ring seed `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` (route B1, `M⊗_R Γ(V)` base change) | enlarge the cover-system BASIS (not a restriction functor) — stays AMBIENT, no `U.Modules→V.Modules`; mirror `affine_tildeVanishing` swapping `_of_localizationAway`→`_of_affineOpen` + `hcov`→`IsAffineOpen`; `Algebra Γ(V) Γ(D a)` NOT synthesizable → `letI` restriction-map `.toAlgebra`+`IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq rfl` | the naive open-subscheme `j⁻¹V≅SpecΓ(j⁻¹V)` transport is the restriction-injectives WALL — REJECTED; the sound route is basis-enlargement + section-level seed |

## Routes

### Route A — acyclic-resolution comparison (CHOSEN)
The augmented Čech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` on `X` (`Cᵖ = ∏ (j_s)_*(F|_{U_s})`) is
(i) a resolution of `F` and (ii) termwise `(pushforward f)`-acyclic. The P4 abstract lemma
gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱf_* F` directly — ONE abstract lemma, NO spectral sequences. Acyclicity
input (ii) reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(affine, qcoh)=0`, supplied by the P3b bridge.

### Route SS — two spectral sequences (REJECTED)
Both absent from Mathlib (multi-thousand-LOC); rests on the same `injective_cech_acyclic` brick as A.
(Named "Route SS" to avoid collision with the 01I8-internal "Route B" section-localization route below.)

### The acyclicity bridge (torsor-free, load-bearing)
Route A's acyclicity reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(Spec A, qcoh)=0` (02KG). The honest
non-circular route is the minimal Čech↔derived bridge: (1) `injective_cech_acyclic`, (2)
`ses_cech_h1`, (3) the dimension-shift `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO) consuming the
standard-cover Čech vanishing of P3 as its condition (3). All three bricks of (1)+(2) are done;
01EO is the next target. This breaks the circular regress: P3 produces standard-cover Čech
vanishing; the bridge lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing.

### `cechAugmented_exact` — sections/sheafification route (P5a resolution input)
The augmented Čech complex is a resolution of `F` (ANY `O_X`-module, ANY open cover; qcoh/affineness
are needed only by the downstream f_*-acyclicity, not by this exactness). Route: reflect `IsZero(Hᵖ)`
through the faithful additive `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` (3-line `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero`+`map_injective`
helper — `reflects_exact_of_faithful` needs `[Abelian]`, avoid); the homology SHEAF = sheafification of
the presheaf homology (`homologyIsoSheafify`, built); the presheaf homology is **locally zero on the
basis `{V ⊆ some Uᵢ}`** because the restricted cover `{Uₛ∩V}` then has a member `=V`, giving the
cover-agnostic prepend-`i_fix` contracting homotopy (`CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy`/`combHomotopy_spec`,
F-valued objectwise analogue), so its sheafification vanishes via `sheafify_kills_locally_zero` + the
`toSheaf∘sheafify ≅ presheafToSheaf∘forget` square (`PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf`).
DEAD ends: stalk functor (no `SheafOfModules.stalk`); the tilde/standard-cover vanishing as the local
discharger (wrong cover — that's the 02KG tool); the naive "section complex exact over each affine V"
(CIRCULAR = `Ȟᵖ(V,·)`≠0). Analogist: `analogies/tosheaf-reflect.md`.
The local discharge's only hard piece (**Sub-brick A**, the per-degree section identification
`Γ(V,pushPullObj F Yₚ)≅∏_σ Γ(U_σ∩V,F)`) is decomposed into the new shared
`CechSectionIdentification.lean`: ONE new-infra leaf (coproduct→product of module sheaves) + four
off-the-shelf pieces + differential via `sectionCech_objD_apply` + Sub-brick-B contractibility via the
de-privatized `Dependent` engine (prepend `i_fix`, identity since `V≤coverOpen i`). Analogist:
`analogies/subbrickA.md`.

### Absolute cohomology realization — Ext of the corepresenting object (Form B)
`H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{X.Modules}(jShriekOU U, F)` where `jShriekOU U := sheafify(free(yoneda U))`
is the corepresenting object of `F ↦ Γ(U,F) = F(U)` (`= j_!O_U` up to iso), built from the
already-shipped `freeYonedaHomEquiv` + `sheafificationAdjunction` (~50–80 LOC). The three facts
01EO consumes are then off-the-shelf with the SES staying in `X.Modules`: injective vanishing
`Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (injective `I` as the 2nd arg — no restriction taken), covariant LES
`Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` at fixed 1st arg `jShriekOU U`, and `H⁰ ≅ Γ(U,F)` via the
corepresentability chain. The discarded alternatives: Form A `Ext(O_U, F|_U)` needs
restriction-preserves-injectives (≈ a 200–500 LOC `j_!` functor); `Functor.rightDerived` has
free injective vanishing but no packaged LES; `Sheaf.H` is absent. Reversal signal: Ext
universe/smallness pain over `SheafOfModules` → fall back to Route γ (Čech colimit), never `Sheaf.H`.

## Open strategic questions

- **Need#1 necessity — RESOLVED (iter-059, strategy-critic).** `affine_serre_vanishing_general_open` is
  fixed to ambient `Spec R` (its proof rests on tilde sheaves `~M`, 01I8 `F≅~ΓF`, section-localization —
  none available for an abstract affine scheme). The open-immersion `_acyclic` leaf is the STANDALONE
  `Ext_{U.Modules}(jShriekOU(j⁻¹W), H)=0` with `U` an ABSTRACT affine scheme (the source of `j`, not
  definitionally `Spec` of anything). So it CANNOT be discharged by re-instantiating Need#2 at
  ambient=`U`; the module-category-equivalence transport `U.Modules ≌ (SpecΓU).Modules`
  (`modulesIsoSpecExtTransport` = Need#1) is the necessary and only available bridge to land on `Spec ΓU`
  where Need#2 applies. Need#1 stays the route.
- **EnoughInjectives connector (cone obligation).** The frozen target provides
  `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` but the 01EO/02KG cone carries the stronger
  `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]`. Connector `HasInjectiveResolutions C → EnoughInjectives C` is TRUE,
  ~6 LOC (deg-0 mono into `I.cocomplex.X 0` via `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι`).
  Build + register at the P5b assembly lane — DISSOLVES the "EnoughInjectives absent in Mathlib" worry.
- P5a last-mile bridge `Hᵏ((f_*I^•)(V)) = Ext^k(jShriek(f⁻¹V), G)`: Ext-by-injective-resolution-of-2nd-arg
  backing CONFIRMED present (`InjectiveResolution.extEquivCohomologyClass`). Due when P5a consumers dispatch.
- Chapter cleanup (non-blocking): `lem:qcoh_localized_sections` is circular by the old span-cover
  mechanism but DORMANT (no DAG path to the main theorem); re-route or delete in a future writer pass.

## Mathlib gaps & new material

Gaps to fill (project-side, Mathlib lacks):
- Standard-cover Čech complex exactness `0→M→∏M_{f_i}→⋯` (P3) — done via L1+L3 around `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
- Presheaf-level Čech machinery for `O_X`-modules (P3b free/section complexes, `injective_cech_acyclic`,
  `ses_cech_h1`) — done. Dropped as off-critical-path: presheaf enough-injectives + δ-functor universality.
- Absolute module-valued `Hⁿ(U,F)` = `Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` via `Abelian.Ext` (Form B); corepresenting
  object `jShriekOU = sheafify(free(yoneda U))`. NOT a bespoke `j_!` functor (that is absent, 200–500 LOC).
- Relative/open-immersion affine vanishing `R^q(jₛ)_*=0` (P5a-consumer). Sound route = Need#2
  enlarge `affineCoverSystem` basis to all affine opens (general-affine-open Serre vanishing,
  no restriction functor) + Need#1 whole-scheme `U≅SpecΓU` Ext transport. The open-subscheme
  `j⁻¹V≅SpecΓ(j⁻¹V)` transport is REJECTED (= restriction-preserves-injectives wall, analogist iter-056).
  CLARIFICATION (avoid re-deriving the wall): the Ext that Need#1 transports along `U≅SpecΓU` is the
  STANDALONE `Ext_{U.Modules}` (cohomology of `U` as its own scheme, via an EQUIVALENCE of module
  categories → `Ext.mapExactFunctor`), NOT the ambient `Ext_{X.Modules}(jShriekOU V,-)`; the
  ambient↔concrete bridge is the 01EO Čech comparison + the section-level seed `htilde`, never an
  ambient→restricted Ext map. Need#2's seed `htilde` is built at the SECTION (module) level for this reason.
- EnoughInjectives connector `HasInjectiveResolutions C → EnoughInjectives C` (~6 LOC, instance) — see
  Open questions; makes the 01EO/02KG cone type-check against the frozen target's weaker hypothesis.
- `cechAugmented_exact` (P5a resolution input): the toSheaf→`homologyIsoSheafify`→`sheafify_kills_locally_zero`
  bridge is DONE; residual = Sub-brick A. ONE new sheaf-infra leaf: indexed coproduct→product of module
  sheaves `(q_p)_*(q_p)^*F ≅ ∏_σ (j_σ)_*(j_σ)^*F` (Mathlib has only binary / structure-sheaf —
  `coprodPresheafObjIso`, `isProductOfDisjoint`). Everything else off-the-shelf / reuse. Lives in new
  `CechSectionIdentification.lean`. ~200–350 LOC.

New project material:
- push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `p ↦ p_* p^* F` — hand-rolled.
- `CechNerve`/`CechComplex`; `AcyclicResolution.lean` (P4); P3b free-presheaf + section Čech complexes,
  `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`, `injective_cech_acyclic`, `ses_cech_h1`.
- `CechSectionIdentification.lean` (NEW): the shared Sub-brick A chain — degreewise
  `Γ(V,pushPullObj F Yₚ)≅∏_σ Γ(U_σ∩V,F)` + complex/differential identification + prepend-`i_fix`
  contractibility; reusable by `cechAugmented_exact` and the dead `CechAcyclic.affine`. Its lone new
  primitive is the indexed coproduct→product module-sheaf iso.
- `AbsoluteCohomology.lean`: `jShriekOU`, corepresentability iso, `H^p := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, -)`, H⁰=Γ,
  injective-vanishing + LES wrappers → 01EO `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` → 02KG `affine_serre_vanishing`.
- comparison assembly `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` via Route A.

8839	  \(\mathcal{H} \mapsto \mathcal{H}|_{j^{-1}(V)}\), whose derived comparison
8840	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^q_{U}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}, \mathcal{H}) \cong H^q(j^{-1}(V),
8841	  \mathcal{H}|_{j^{-1}(V)})\) is exactly ``restriction preserves injectives'' --- infrastructure not
8842	  available in the present formalism. The sound route therefore keeps the spectrum isomorphism at the
8843	  level of the \emph{whole} affine \(U\) as in~(2b) and handles the general affine open ambiently
8844	  via~(2c).
8845	
8846	  \emph{(3) Locally zero implies zero sheafification.} The
8847	  passage from ``\(H^k(\cdots) = 0\) on a basis of affine opens'' to ``\(R^k f_* = 0\)'' is the
8848	  module-sheafification analogue of Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero} (and of its packaged
8849	  form Lemma~\ref{lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero}). It is obtained by transporting the
8850	  abelian-sheaf statement across the natural isomorphism
8851	  \(\operatorname{toSheaf} \circ \operatorname{sheafify} \cong \operatorname{presheafToSheaf} \circ
8852	  \operatorname{forget}\) between module-sheafification followed by the forgetful functor and abelian
8853	  sheafification; the ``locally zero'' hypothesis it consumes is exactly the basis-of-affines vanishing
8854	  supplied by identifications (1) and~(2) above.
8855	\end{proof}
8856	
8857	\begin{lemma}
8858	[The pushforward-sections functor and its additivity]
8859	  \label{lem:pushforward_sections_functor}
8860	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardSectionsFunctor,
8861	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardSectionsFunctor_additive}
8862	  \uses{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
8863	  Let \(j : U \to X\) be a morphism and \(W \subseteq X\) an open. The
8864	  \emph{pushforward-sections functor} \(\operatorname{pushforwardSectionsFunctor} j\,W :
8865	  U.\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{Ab}\) sends an \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module \(M\) to its sections
8866	  \(\Gamma(W, j_* M) = \Gamma(j^{-1}(W), M)\) --- the pushforward along \(j\), forgotten to
8867	  abelian groups, viewed as a presheaf and evaluated at \(W\). It is an additive functor, and
8868	  its \(q\)-th right derived functor applied to \(H\) computes the absolute cohomology
8869	  \(H^q(j^{-1}(W), H)\).

9640	  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes and \(H : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) a
9641	  quasi-coherent module. Then the pushforward \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\) along
9642	  \(\varphi\) is again quasi-coherent.

9644	\begin{proof}
9645	  \uses{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib,
9646	    lem:presentation_map_mathlib, lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib,
9647	    lem:nonempty_quasicoherentData_mathlib, lem:presentation_ofIsIso_mathlib,
9648	    lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}
9649	  Quasi-coherence is a \emph{local} datum: it transports across the equivalence \(\Phi\) by moving
9650	  \(H\)'s local presentations to the image cover, member by member. We never seek a global presentation
9651	  of \(H\) (it has none — \(H\) is quasi-coherent only on the abstract source); we move the local
9652	  presentations one open at a time.
9653	
9654	  \emph{The cover.} Extract \(H\)'s quasi-coherence datum
9655	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:nonempty_quasicoherentData_mathlib}): a family \((U_i)_{i \in I}\) of opens covering
9656	  \(X\) together with, for each \(i\), a presentation \(p_i\) of \(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\). Form the
9657	  image cover \(V_i = \varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,U_i\) of \(Y\); it covers \(Y\) because the underlying
9658	  map of \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}\) is a homeomorphism, so the preimages of a cover form a cover (the image
9659	  of an affine open under the scheme isomorphism is again affine,
9660	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}, when one wants the cover to remain affine). By
9661	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib} it suffices to show that
9662	  \((\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H).\operatorname{over} V_i\) is quasi-coherent for every \(i\).
9663	
9664	  \emph{Per-member transport.} Fix \(i\) and let
9665	  \(e_i : (X.\mathrm{Modules}\!\restriction\! U_i) \simeq (Y.\mathrm{Modules}\!\restriction\! V_i)\) be
9666	  the opens-site equivalence induced by \(\varphi\) restricted to \(U_i\), built from
9667	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}. This \(e_i\) preserves colimits and carries
9668	  the structure-sheaf unit to the structure-sheaf unit (it is a site equivalence whose underlying base
9669	  is a homeomorphism), so by Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib} the presentation \(p_i\) of
9670	  \(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\) transports to a presentation of
9671	  \(e_i.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}(H.\operatorname{over} U_i)\). The comparison isomorphism of
9672	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction},
9673	  \[
9674	    e_i.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\bigr)
9675	      \;\cong\;
9676	    \bigl(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\bigr).\operatorname{over} V_i,
9677	  \]
9678	  then carries that presentation, via Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_ofIsIso_mathlib}, to a presentation of
9679	  \((\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H).\operatorname{over} V_i\). A sheaf of modules with a (global)
9680	  presentation is quasi-coherent, so each member of the image cover is quasi-coherent, and the claim
9681	  follows by Lemma~\ref{lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib}.
9682	
9683	  This is the isomorphism case of the Stacks functoriality remark (``it is in general not the case that
9684	  the pushforward of a quasi-coherent module is quasi-coherent''; here it holds because \(\varphi\) is an
9685	  isomorphism, equivalently \(\varphi_* = (\varphi^{-1})^*\) is a pullback).
9686	\end{proof}
